hints.to go: That would be 'ire' — 'Sunt' comes from 'esse' (to be)
hints.to know: That would be 'scire' — 'Sunt' comes from 'esse' (to be)
hints.to follow: That would be 'sequi' — 'Sunt' comes from 'esse' (to be)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sint' — 'sunt' is present indicative
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'fuerunt' — 'sunt' is present indicative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'erunt' — 'sunt' is present indicative

hints.eo, ire, ii, itum: These are the parts of 'ire' (to go)
hints.do, dare, dedi, datum: These are the parts of 'dare' (to give)
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the parts of 'stare' (to stand)

hints.genitive: Genitive singular is also 'lacrimae', but here it is nominative plural, subject of 'sunt'
hints.dative: Dative singular is also 'lacrimae', but context demands nominative plural with 'sunt'
hints.vocative: Vocative would be unusual for 'tears' — 'lacrimae' is nominative plural

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'lacrimae' ends in -ae (1st declension)
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'lacrimae' ends in -ae
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'lacrimae' ends in -ae

hints.accusative: Accusative plural would be 'res' — 'rerum' with -um is genitive plural
hints.nominative: Nominative singular is 'res' — 'rerum' is genitive plural
hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'rebus' — 'rerum' is genitive plural

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'rei' ends in -ei (5th declension)
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'rei' ends in -ei
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'rei' ends in -ei

steps.reveal: mentem = the mind/heart (acc. sg., object of 'tangunt')

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'mens' — the -em ending marks the accusative
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'mente' — 'mentem' with -em is accusative
hints.dative: Dative would be 'menti' — 'mentem' is accusative, object of 'tangunt'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'mentis' ends in -is (3rd declension)
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'mentis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'mentis' ends in -is

hints.to hold: That would be 'tenere' — 'tangunt' comes from 'tangere' (to touch)
hints.to weave: That would be 'texere' — 'tangunt' comes from 'tangere' (to touch)
hints.to be silent: That would be 'tacere' — 'tangunt' comes from 'tangere' (to touch)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'tangant' — 'tangunt' with -u- is present indicative (3rd conj.)
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'tetigerunt' — 'tangunt' is present indicative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'tangent' — 'tangunt' is present indicative

hints.teneo, tenere, tenui, tentum: These are the parts of 'tenere' (to hold)
hints.texo, texere, texui, textum: These are the parts of 'texere' (to weave)
hints.taceo, tacere, tacui, tacitum: These are the parts of 'tacere' (to be silent)

translation: There are tears for things, and mortal affairs touch the mind
